subject: Two dark worm-like creatures with circular toothed mouths rising in a swamp or subterranean environment
description: Two segmented worm-like creatures rear up from a dark, foggy environment. Each has a circular, disc-shaped head with a ring of teeth or small spines around a central mouth — like a lamprey or leech magnified many times. The bodies are heavily ribbed or segmented in a dark bronze-brown tone. The background is thick with dark fog and vague organic shapes — hanging roots, moss, or other subterranean vegetation — in near-total darkness. The two creatures face slightly different directions, with one tilted more toward the viewer than the other. The palette is almost entirely desaturated dark browns and blacks with slight green and grey tones.
foreground: Two segmented worm-creatures with circular toothed maws rearing up
background: Dense dark fog with organic shapes suggesting subterranean or swamp environment
